purpose: Move from requirements analysis to relational ERD/table design, construction, implementation, and tuning feedback.
required_context: business objective and operational subject areas
OLTP, warehouse, reporting, or hybrid workload
expected queries, transactions, concurrency, growth, and service windows
budget, timeline, skills, and maintenance constraints
ask_if_missing: Is this database primarily OLTP, analytical/warehouse, or hybrid?
What are the highest-value user workflows and reports?
assume_if_low_risk: start with analysis and ERD-level design before vendor-specific DDL
separate operational and analytical workloads when their needs conflict

branches.criteria: new relational application with unclear requirements
branches.recommendation: perform requirements analysis, identify subject-area tables, draw ERD, then refine fields, keys, datatypes, and constraints

branches.criteria: existing database, spreadsheet, or paper process is being replaced
branches.recommendation: use old artifacts as evidence, identify why the old system fails, then design the target structure from required operations and rules

branches.criteria: performance and reporting are central from the beginning
branches.recommendation: classify OLTP vs warehouse needs early, because workload determines normalization, dimensional modeling, indexing, and physical design

failure_modes: starting with DDL before requirements and ERD analysis
copying old structures without diagnosing old-system failures
interviewing only technical staff and missing end-user operations
ignoring performance until after table structure is locked
using generic industry models without pruning irrelevant scope
output_checks: names tables, relationships, and key business rules
captures open questions for users or existing artifacts
